lyrics.jp: 大丈夫、痛みにとっても弱いから
lyrics.romaji: Daijoubu, itami ni tottemo yowai kara
lyrics.en: It's okay, because I'm very weak when it comes to pain

breakdown.meaning: very, extremely (casual form of とても)

learn.grammar: 〜から at the end of a clause means 'because'. It gives the reason for what came before. Pattern: [reason]から、[result].
learn.literal: Okay — toward pain very weak, because

example.jp: 疲れているから、早く寝る。
example.romaji: Tsukarete iru kara, hayaku neru.
example.en: Because I'm tired, I'll go to bed early.

lyrics.jp: 大丈夫、憧れは時に憧れのまんま
lyrics.romaji: Daijoubu, akogare wa toki ni akogare no manma
lyrics.en: It's okay, sometimes yearnings just stay as yearnings

breakdown.meaning: as-is, just as it is (casual form of まま)

learn.grammar: 〜のまま / まんま means 'remaining as it is, unchanged'. The word 憧れ repeats (yearning…yearning) for poetic emphasis — a technique called 畳語 (jougo, reduplication).
learn.literal: Okay — yearning sometimes as yearning's as-is

example.jp: 夢は夢のままでいい。
example.romaji: Yume wa yume no mama de ii.
example.en: It's okay for a dream to stay just a dream.

lyrics.jp: 愛、ジョーク、それとたまにキツめのネガティブ
lyrics.romaji: Ai, jooku, sore to tama ni kitsume no negatibu
lyrics.en: Love, jokes, and sometimes a touch of harsh negativity

learn.grammar: 〜め is a suffix meaning 'somewhat [adj]' or 'on the [adj] side'. キツい (harsh) → キツめ (a bit harsh). It softens the intensity.
learn.literal: Love, joke, and occasionally somewhat-harsh negativity

example.jp: 今日は早めに起きた。
example.romaji: Kyou wa hayame ni okita.
example.en: Today I woke up a bit on the early side.

lyrics.jp: それでいつも元通りさ
lyrics.romaji: Sore de itsumo moto doori sa
lyrics.en: And yet we always go back to how we were

breakdown.meaning: and so, with that, and yet
back to the original state, back to normal

learn.grammar: さ as a sentence-ending particle adds a casual, slightly resigned tone — 'that's just how it is, you know'. Softer and more reflective than よ.
learn.literal: With that, always back-to-original — you know

example.jp: 喧嘩しても、いつも元通りさ。
example.romaji: Kenka shite mo, itsumo moto doori sa.
example.en: Even after a fight, we always go back to normal, you know.

lyrics.jp: 君と僕との間柄なら
lyrics.romaji: Kimi to boku to no aidagara nara
lyrics.en: Given the relationship between you and me

breakdown.meaning: the nature of a relationship, how two people relate
if it's, given that (sets up a premise)

learn.grammar: 〜なら is a conditional meaning 'if it's [X] / given that [X]'. It accepts a situation as premise and draws a conclusion from it. Different from たら (event-based) — なら is about framing context.
learn.literal: You and me's relationship, if it's that

example.jp: 友達なら、助け合うのは当然だ。
example.romaji: Tomodachi nara, tasuke au no wa touzen da.
example.en: If you're friends, it's natural to help each other.

lyrics.jp: そりゃ出くわす問題も様々
lyrics.romaji: Sorya dekuwasu mondai mo samazama
lyrics.en: Of course the problems we run into are all kinds

breakdown.meaning: of course, naturally (casual form of それは)
to run into, to encounter unexpectedly

learn.grammar: そりゃ (< それは) introduces a self-evident conclusion: 'of course, naturally'. Combined with なら in the previous line: 'given X relationship, of course Y follows'.
learn.literal: Of-course encounter problems also various

example.jp: 長く付き合えば、問題も様々出てくる。
example.romaji: Nagaku tsukiaeba, mondai mo samazama dete kuru.
example.en: In a long relationship, all kinds of problems come up.

lyrics.jp: だから隣に居りゃ 旅の道すがら
lyrics.romaji: Dakara tonari ni irya tabi no michisugara
lyrics.en: So as long as you're beside me on this journey

breakdown.meaning: along the way, while on the road

learn.grammar: 〜いれば contracts to 〜いりゃ / 居りゃ in casual speech and song lyrics. 居れば → 居りゃ = 'if [you] are there'. This line continues into the next — the thought spans two lines.
learn.literal: That's why, beside if-there, journey's along-the-way

example.jp: 隣に居てくれるから、怖くない。
example.romaji: Tonari ni ite kureru kara, kowakunai.
example.en: Because you're beside me, I'm not scared.

lyrics.jp: どんなヤミからも命からがら
lyrics.romaji: Donna yami kara mo inochi karagara
lyrics.en: We'll barely escape from any kind of darkness

breakdown.meaning: any kind of, whatever kind of
even from (から = from, も = even)
barely escaping with one's life, by the skin of one's teeth

learn.grammar: 命からがら is a fixed expression (慣用句) meaning 'barely with one's life'. Writing ヤミ in katakana instead of 闇 (kanji) is a stylistic choice — katakana signals intensity or an alien/looming quality.
learn.literal: Any darkness even from, barely-with-one's-life

example.jp: 事故から命からがら逃げた。
example.romaji: Jiko kara inochi karagara nigeta.
example.en: I barely escaped from the accident with my life.

lyrics.jp: 助けるよ 助けてよ My Buddy, Darling
lyrics.romaji: Tasukeru yo tasukete yo My Buddy, Darling
lyrics.en: I'll save you, save me, My Buddy, Darling

breakdown.meaning: to help, to save, to rescue [verb]

learn.grammar: The て-form of a verb used alone is a request or plea: 助けて = 'please help me!'. The same verb appears twice — first asserting (I will save you) then pleading (save me). This mirrors the mutual nature of the relationship.
learn.literal: Will-save [asserting]! Save-me [pleading]! My Buddy, Darling

example.jp: 助けてください！道に迷いました。
example.romaji: Tasukete kudasai! Michi ni mayoimashita.
example.en: Please help me! I got lost.

lyrics.jp: SOSが不器用でも 気にしないからもう
lyrics.romaji: SOS ga bukiyou demo ki ni shinai kara mou
lyrics.en: Even if your SOS is clumsy, I don't mind anymore

breakdown.meaning: SOS — emergency signal, cry for help
already, anymore — signals a firm decision

learn.grammar: 〜でも means 'even if' — it concedes a point while dismissing its importance. もう here signals emotional finality: 'I've decided I won't mind'. Compare: もう食べた (already ate) vs もう気にしない (done caring — it's decided).
learn.literal: SOS subject clumsy even-if, don't-mind because, now

example.jp: 下手でも、一生懸命やっていれば気にしない。
example.romaji: Heta demo, isshoukenmei yatte ireba ki ni shinai.
example.en: Even if you're not good at it, I don't mind as long as you're trying hard.

lyrics.jp: そっけないくらいで僕らは丁度良いんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Sokkenai kurai de bokura wa choudo ii n janai?
lyrics.en: Isn't it just right for us to be a little cold and distant?

breakdown.meaning: at the level of, to that extent (degree marker)

learn.grammar: 〜くらいで means 'at the level of [X]'. Here: 'being THIS cold is just the right amount for us'. 〜んじゃない？ is a common spoken rhetorical question — softer and more inviting than でしょう？
learn.literal: Cold-and-indifferent level-of, we just-right isn't-it?

example.jp: これくらいの距離感がちょうどいい。
example.romaji: Kore kurai no kyori-kan ga choudo ii.
example.en: This level of distance between us is just right.

lyrics.jp: きっと涙も言葉もおまけでも良いんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Kitto namida mo kotoba mo omake demo ii n janai?
lyrics.en: Surely it's fine if tears and words are just extras, right?

breakdown.meaning: bonus, extra, freebie — something nice but not essential

learn.grammar: 〜でもいい means 'it's fine even if it's [X]'. おまけ (bonus/extra) suggests that words and tears are nice but not required — the connection exists beyond expression.
learn.literal: Surely tears also words also bonus even-if okay isn't-it?

example.jp: うまく言えなくてもいいんじゃない？
example.romaji: Umaku ienakute mo iin janai?
example.en: Isn't it okay even if you can't say it well?

lyrics.jp: 多分世界が次のフェーズへ行こうと見つけてみせるよ
lyrics.romaji: Tabun sekai ga tsugi no feezu e ikou to mitsukete miseru yo
lyrics.en: As the world moves on to the next phase, I'll make sure we find it

breakdown.meaning: probably, maybe (less certain than きっと)
let's go, going to go (volitional of 行く)
will definitely find — just watch me (みつける + てみせる)

learn.grammar: 〜てみせる = 'I will definitely do X, just watch me'. Expresses strong determination. みつける (find) + てみせる = 'I will definitely find it'. Add よ for extra emphasis.
learn.literal: Probably world next phase toward going, will-definitely-find (emphasis)

example.jp: 絶対に成功してみせるよ。
example.romaji: Zettai ni seikou shite miseru yo.
example.en: I'll definitely succeed — just watch me.

lyrics.jp: いや嫌でも見つかるんだろうよ
lyrics.romaji: Iya, iya demo mitsukaru n darou yo
lyrics.en: Actually, it'll turn up whether we like it or not

breakdown.meaning: no, actually, well — discourse marker walking back the previous statement
whether we like it or not, inevitably
to be found, to turn up (intransitive)

learn.grammar: 見つかる (intransitive: to be found) vs 見つける (transitive: to find). The previous line said 'I'll find it!' — this line gently corrects with 'actually it'll just be found on its own'. 嫌でも = 'even if reluctant / whether you like it or not'.
learn.literal: Actually, even-if-reluctant, will-be-found probably (emphasis)

example.jp: 嫌でも、いつか向き合わなければならない。
example.romaji: Iya demo, itsuka mukiawa nakereba naranai.
example.en: Whether we like it or not, we'll have to face it someday.

lyrics.jp: あっけないくらい早い時間も良いんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Akkenai kurai hayai jikan mo ii n janai?
lyrics.en: Isn't it fine that time passes by so surprisingly fast?

breakdown.meaning: surprisingly brief, over before you know it, anticlimactically quick
to the extent of, as much as
isn't it okay? isn't it fine?

learn.grammar: あっけない is a rich adjective meaning 'disappointingly quick, over before you were ready'. あっけないくらい = 'to a surprisingly quick degree'. It often carries gentle wistfulness.
learn.literal: Surprisingly-quick to-the-extent-of fast time also okay isn't-it?

example.jp: 楽しい時間はあっけないくらい過ぎてしまう。
example.romaji: Tanoshii jikan wa akkenai kurai sugite shimau.
example.en: Fun times pass by surprisingly quickly.

lyrics.jp: きっとありふれてないくらいが大切なんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Kitto arifurete nai kurai ga taisetsu na n janai?
lyrics.en: Surely something so uncommon is exactly what makes it precious, right?

breakdown.meaning: not commonplace, not ordinary, rare (negative of ありふれる)
it is precisely that extent which...

learn.grammar: ありふれる means 'to be commonplace, to be everywhere'. ありふれてない = 'not commonplace, rare'. 〜くらいが大切 = 'it's precisely that rarity which matters'.
learn.literal: Surely not-commonplace extent is precious isn't-it?

example.jp: ありふれてない経験だからこそ、大切にしたい。
example.romaji: Arifurete nai keiken dakara koso, taisetsu ni shitai.
example.en: It's precisely because it's a rare experience that I want to treasure it.

lyrics.jp: なんてさ 本気で思っちゃう心の恥ずかしさが僕らだけにあるTATTOO
lyrics.romaji: Nante sa honki de omochau kokoro no hazukashisa ga bokura dake ni aru TATTOO
lyrics.en: Things like that — the embarrassment of genuinely believing it is a TATTOO that belongs only to us

breakdown.meaning: things like that, you know — casual dismissal + さ particle
can't help but think (思う + ちゃう)
only in us, belonging to us alone
tattoo — the title word, a permanent mark

learn.grammar: 〜ちゃう (contracted from てしまう) = 'end up doing / can't help but do'. 思っちゃう = 'I can't help but think it'. This marks the feeling as involuntary. This is the title line: the embarrassing sincerity of this bond IS the tattoo.
learn.literal: Things like that — genuinely end-up-thinking, heart's embarrassment that only in us exists TATTOO

example.jp: つい笑っちゃった。
example.romaji: Tsui waracchaetta.
example.en: I couldn't help but laugh.

lyrics.jp: 消えない 消さない 消させやしない
lyrics.romaji: Kienai kesanai kesase ya shinai
lyrics.en: Won't fade, won't erase it, won't let it be erased

breakdown.meaning: won't fade/disappear (negative of 消える — intransitive)
won't erase (negative of 消す — transitive)
absolutely won't let it be erased (causative + emphatic negative)

learn.grammar: 〜やしない is an emphatic negative: 消させやしない = 'I absolutely will not allow it to be erased'. The three-part escalation covers all bases: intransitive (it fades) → transitive (I erase) → causative (someone makes it erased).
learn.literal: Won't-fade, won't-erase, absolutely-won't-allow-to-be-erased

example.jp: 誰にも消させやしない。
example.romaji: Dare ni mo kesase ya shinai.
example.en: I won't let anyone erase it.

lyrics.jp: 大丈夫、見せびらかす必要はないから
lyrics.romaji: Daijoubu, misebikasu hitsuyou wa nai kara
lyrics.en: It's okay, because there's no need to show it off

breakdown.meaning: to show off, to flaunt, to display ostentatiously

learn.grammar: 〜必要はない = 'there is no need to [verb]'. Pattern: [verb plain form] + 必要はない. 見せびらかす is expressive: 見せる (show) + びらかす (spread/flaunt) = to show off proudly.
learn.literal: Okay — flaunt necessity doesn't-exist because

example.jp: わざわざ見せびらかす必要はない。
example.romaji: Wazawaza misebikasu hitsuyou wa nai.
example.en: There's no need to go out of your way to show it off.

lyrics.jp: 大丈夫、自分たちだけ分かっていりゃいいから
lyrics.romaji: Daijoubu, jibuntachi dake wakatte irya ii kara
lyrics.en: It's okay, because it's enough that only we understand

breakdown.meaning: ourselves, we ourselves (自分 + たち plural)

learn.grammar: 〜ていりゃいい (contracted from ていればいい) = 'it's enough to be [doing X]'. Pattern: [て-form]いればいい = 'all you need is to be doing X'. だけ narrows it: 'just us understanding is enough'.
learn.literal: Okay — ourselves only, understanding if-are, that's-enough because

example.jp: 自分たちだけ分かっていれば十分だ。
example.romaji: Jibuntachi dake wakatte ireba juubun da.
example.en: It's enough if only we understand.

lyrics.jp: ハイボール、爆笑の渦に呑まれるネガティブ
lyrics.romaji: Haibooru, bakushou no uzu ni nomareru negatibu
lyrics.en: Highballs, negativity swept up in a whirlpool of laughter

breakdown.meaning: highball — whisky + soda, a popular Japanese drink
to be swept up in, to be swallowed (passive of 呑む)

learn.grammar: 呑まれる is the passive of 呑む (to swallow). Here: negativity gets swallowed by the laughter — it's overwhelmed. Compare verse 1's キツめのネガティブ (harsh negativity) — in verse 2, negativity is absorbed into laughter. The mood has shifted.
learn.literal: Highball, roaring-laughter's whirlpool into, swept-up negativity

example.jp: 笑いの渦に呑まれて、悩みを忘れた。
example.romaji: Warai no uzu ni nomarete, nayami wo wasureta.
example.en: Swept up in laughter, I forgot my worries.

lyrics.jp: まるで まるで別次元さ
lyrics.romaji: Marude marude betsu jigen sa
lyrics.en: It's like, it's like a whole different dimension

breakdown.meaning: just like, as if, it's as though (sets up a comparison)
a completely different dimension, a whole other level

learn.grammar: まるで usually pairs with よう/みたい: まるで〜のようだ = 'it's just like [X]'. Here the comparison is left open — まるで別次元さ — dropping the よう for a more direct effect. Repetition (まるで まるで) conveys stunned, searching speech.
learn.literal: Just-like, just-like, different-dimension — you know

example.jp: まるで別次元にいるみたいだ。
example.romaji: Marude betsu jigen ni iru mitai da.
example.en: It's like being in a completely different dimension.

lyrics.jp: そしてそんな君と僕との間柄なら
lyrics.romaji: Soshite sonna kimi to boku to no aidagara nara
lyrics.en: And given a relationship like that between you and me

breakdown.meaning: that kind of, like that (references what was just described)

learn.grammar: そんな references what came before: 'that kind of relationship just described'. This verse 2 version adds そして (and then) and そんな (that kind of), giving it more weight.
learn.literal: And, that-kind-of you and me's relationship, given that

example.jp: そんな関係なら、何でも話せるはずだ。
example.romaji: Sonna kankei nara, nandemo hanaseru hazu da.
example.en: If it's that kind of relationship, you should be able to talk about anything.

lyrics.jp: そりゃ赤らんだ顔だって晒すわな
lyrics.romaji: Sorya akararanda kao datte sarasu wa na
lyrics.en: Of course we'd show each other our flushed, embarrassed faces

breakdown.meaning: flushed red, blushed (past of 赤らむ)
even (emphatic particle — 'even something as embarrassing as')
to expose, to reveal, to show (even something private)
sentence-ending: gentle self-evident confirmation (わ + な)

learn.grammar: わな at end of sentence (わ + な) expresses self-evident resignation or confirmation: 'of course that's how it would be'. だって highlights that even embarrassing things get revealed — this is genuine intimacy.
learn.literal: Of-course flushed-face even, expose — naturally-of-course

example.jp: そんな仲なら、恥ずかしい顔も晒せる。
example.romaji: Sonna naka nara, hazukashii kao mo sarasu koto ga dekiru.
example.en: If you're that close, you can show even your embarrassing face.

lyrics.jp: 今は例えるとか うまく出来ないけどさ
lyrics.romaji: Ima wa tatoeru toka umaku dekinai kedo sa
lyrics.en: I can't quite put it into words right now, but

breakdown.meaning: right now, at this point in time
things like finding a metaphor, trying to put it into words
but — soft trailing contrast, 'but you know...'

learn.grammar: 〜とか = 'things like [X]' — vague listing. 例えるとか = 'things like putting it into words'. けどさ is a common spoken ending that trails off — 'but you know...' — implying the thought continues even when unspoken.
learn.literal: Right-now things-like-putting-into-words, well can't-do, but — you know

example.jp: うまく言えないけど、大切だと思ってる。
example.romaji: Umaku ienai kedo, taisetsu da to omotteiru.
example.en: I can't say it well, but I think you're important to me.

lyrics.jp: かけがえない類いの何かさながら
lyrics.romaji: Kakegae nai tagui no nanika sanagara
lyrics.en: It's like something truly irreplaceable

breakdown.meaning: kind, type, the likes of (rare, literary word)
just like, as if, exactly like (literary/poetic adverb)

learn.grammar: さながら is a literary/poetic adverb meaning 'just like / exactly as if'. More formal and evocative than まるで. かけがえない is a compound: かけ (substitute/exchange) + がえない (can't be done) = cannot be replaced.
learn.literal: Irreplaceable kind's something, just-like

example.jp: 君はかけがえない存在だ。
example.romaji: Kimi wa kakegae nai sonzai da.
example.en: You are an irreplaceable presence to me.

lyrics.jp: リスペクト 増してくようなDaily Feeling
lyrics.romaji: Risupekuto mashite ku you na Daily Feeling
lyrics.en: A daily feeling like respect that keeps on growing

breakdown.meaning: keeps growing, increasing (増す + ていく contracted to てく)

learn.grammar: 〜ていく contracts to 〜てく in casual speech and song: 増していく → 増してく = 'keeps growing (progressively)'. This contraction is very common in lyrics and conversation.
learn.literal: Respect keeps-growing like, Daily Feeling

example.jp: 一緒にいるほど、リスペクトが増していく。
example.romaji: Issho ni iru hodo, risupekuto ga mashite iku.
example.en: The more time we spend together, the more my respect grows.

lyrics.jp: 言い合ったりするのにも もう慣れっこだろう
lyrics.romaji: Ii attari suru no ni mo mou narekko darou
lyrics.en: By now we're used to arguing with each other too, aren't we

breakdown.meaning: to argue, to exchange words back and forth
even for / even when it comes to
completely used to, totally accustomed (casual intensified 慣れる)

learn.grammar: 慣れっこ is a colloquial intensified form of 慣れる (get used to). The っこ suffix adds emphasis and casualness: 'totally used to it'. 〜たりする lists an action as one example among others.
learn.literal: Arguing-back-and-forth doing-things-like, even-when, already totally-used-to, right?

example.jp: 言い合いには、もう慣れっこだ。
example.romaji: Ii ai ni wa, mou narekko da.
example.en: I'm totally used to our arguments by now.

lyrics.jp: 情け容赦無い時代のバッドワードが
lyrics.romaji: Nasake yousha nai jidai no baddo waado ga
lyrics.en: The harsh words of a merciless era

learn.grammar: 情け容赦無い combines 情け (compassion), 容赦 (mercy/pardon), and 無い (without). The が subject particle here connects to the verb どれだけ拗れても further on. Japanese sentences can stretch across multiple lines.
learn.literal: Merciless era's bad-words subject [continues...]

example.jp: 情け容赦無い言葉が心を傷つける。
example.romaji: Nasake yousha nai kotoba ga kokoro wo kizutsukeru.
example.en: Merciless words wound the heart.

lyrics.jp: ひょんな時に僕らの事を脅かす時には
lyrics.romaji: Hyon na toki ni bokura no koto wo odokasu toki ni wa
lyrics.en: When they unexpectedly come to threaten us

breakdown.meaning: unexpected, strange, out of nowhere (colloquial adjective)

learn.grammar: ひょんな is a colloquial adjective meaning 'unexpected, out of nowhere'. It only appears before nouns: ひょんなこと (unexpected thing), ひょんな時 (unexpected moment). Continues the sentence from the previous line.
learn.literal: Unexpected time in, us about, threaten, at-such-times

example.jp: ひょんなことから仲良くなった。
example.romaji: Hyon na koto kara naka yoku natta.
example.en: We became close friends out of nowhere.

lyrics.jp: 絡まる充電のコードのように
lyrics.romaji: Karamaru juuden no koodo no you ni
lyrics.en: Like tangled charging cables

learn.grammar: 〜のように / 〜のような is the standard simile pattern: 'like [X]'. This everyday image (tangled phone charger cables) grounds an abstract threat into something everyone recognises — modern, relatable, and charming.
learn.literal: Tangling charging cord's like

example.jp: コードのように絡まった問題を解きほぐす。
example.romaji: Koodo no you ni karamatta mondai wo tokihogusu.
example.en: Untangling the problems like tangled cables.

lyrics.jp: どれだけ拗れても もう意地でも繋ぎ合っていようよ
lyrics.romaji: Dore dake kojirete mo mou iji demo tsunagi atte iyou yo
lyrics.en: No matter how tangled things get, let's stubbornly stay connected

breakdown.meaning: no matter how much, to whatever degree
getting complicated/entangled (て-form of 拗れる)
stubbornly, out of sheer will, no matter what
to stay connected with each other (mutual form)
let's stay [doing this] (volitional of ている)

learn.grammar: 〜ていよう (volitional of ている) = 'let's remain in this state'. 繋ぎ合っていよう = 'let's stay connected to each other'. 意地でも is a strong idiom: 意地 (stubbornness) + でも = 'even with sheer stubbornness / no matter what'.
learn.literal: However-tangled even, stubbornly, let's-stay-connected

example.jp: 意地でも諦めない。
example.romaji: Iji demo akiramenai.
example.en: I won't give up out of sheer stubbornness.

lyrics.jp: しょうもないくらいの方が笑えていいんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Shoumonai kurai no hou ga waraete ii n janai?
lyrics.en: Isn't it better when things are so trivial you can laugh at them?

breakdown.meaning: trivial, pointless, worthless (Kansai dialect origin, now widespread)
it's better when it's at the level of [X]
can laugh, is laughable (potential form て-form)
isn't it better? isn't that good?

learn.grammar: 〜の方が [adjective] = 'it's more [adj] when / it's better that'. くらいの方がいい = 'it's better at that level'. 笑える (can laugh / is laughable) uses the potential form emotionally — being able to laugh at something is a kind of freedom.
learn.literal: Trivial level's-the-one-that can-laugh good isn't-it?

example.jp: しょうもないことで笑えるのが、友達の証だ。
example.romaji: Shoumonai koto de waraeru no ga, tomodachi no akashi da.
example.en: Being able to laugh at trivial things together is proof of friendship.

lyrics.jp: きっと思い出して飲んだ時に美味しすぎんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Kitto omoidashite nonda toki ni umasu gin janai?
lyrics.en: When we drink while reminiscing about this, won't it taste amazing?

breakdown.meaning: remembering, recalling (て-form of 思い出す)
way too delicious (美味しい + すぎ)

learn.grammar: 〜すぎ means 'excessively / way too much'. 美味しすぎ = 'way too delicious'. Often used positively in colloquial speech. This image — drinking while looking back on memories — is quintessentially Japanese: お酒 (sake) and 思い出 (memories) are deeply linked culturally.
learn.literal: Surely remembering, drink-at-that-time, too-delicious won't-it?

example.jp: 懐かしい話をしながら飲むと、美味しすぎる。
example.romaji: Natsukashii hanashi wo shinagara nomu to, umasu giru.
example.en: Drinking while sharing nostalgic stories makes it taste way too good.

lyrics.jp: なんてさ 強がりを何度も互いの背中に
lyrics.romaji: Nante sa tsuyogari wo nandomo otagai no senaka ni
lyrics.en: Things like that — let's stamp our bluster on each other's backs again and again

breakdown.meaning: things like that, you know — casual intro + さ particle
bluster, bravado, putting on a brave face

learn.grammar: 強がり comes from 強がる (to put on a brave face, to bluster). The 〜がり noun form = 'the act of pretending to be strong'. This line continues into the next — 強がりを背中に付ける = stamping bravado onto each other's backs like handprints.
learn.literal: Things like that — bravado, many-times, each-other's back on

example.jp: 強がりを言っても、本当は怖かった。
example.romaji: Tsuyogari wo itte mo, hontou wa kowakatta.
example.en: Even though I acted tough, I was really scared.

lyrics.jp: 手形みたいにやりすぎなぐらい付け合っていよう
lyrics.romaji: Tegata mitai ni yarisugi na gurai tsuke atte iyou
lyrics.en: Like handprints, overdoing it — let's keep marking each other

breakdown.meaning: overdoing it, going too far (やる + すぎ)
to the extent of, as much as that
stamping on each other, marking each other (mutual 付ける)
let's stay / let's keep (volitional of いる)

learn.grammar: 付け合う is 付ける (to attach/stamp) in mutual form (〜合う = doing X to each other). The concrete image: stamping bravado on each other's backs like handprints — やりすぎなぐらい (to the point of overdoing it) makes it joyful and excessive.
learn.literal: Handprint like, overdoing-it to-the-extent-of, marking-each-other let's-keep

example.jp: やりすぎなぐらい練習したから、自信がある。
example.romaji: Yarisugi na gurai renshuu shita kara, jishin ga aru.
example.en: Because I practiced to the point of overdoing it, I'm confident.

lyrics.jp: そっけないくらいで僕らは丁度良いんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Sokkenai kurai de bokura wa choudo ii n janai?
lyrics.en: Isn't it just right for us to be a little cold and distant?

breakdown.meaning: at the level of, to that extent (degree marker)

learn.grammar: Chorus repeat. After the bridge (tangled cables, handprints, bravado), そっけない no longer sounds like emotional distance — it sounds like comfortable shorthand between two people who know each other completely.
learn.literal: Cold-and-indifferent level-of, we just-right isn't-it?

example.jp: これくらいの距離感がちょうどいい。
example.romaji: Kore kurai no kyori-kan ga choudo ii.
example.en: This level of distance between us is just right.

lyrics.jp: きっと涙も言葉もおまけでも良いんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Kitto namida mo kotoba mo omake demo ii n janai?
lyrics.en: Surely it's fine if tears and words are just extras, right?

breakdown.meaning: bonus, extra, freebie — something nice but not essential

learn.grammar: Chorus repeat. The song has now shown so many ways this bond manifests — arguments, blushing faces, handprints — that calling tears and words おまけ (extras) feels earned rather than dismissive.
learn.literal: Surely tears also words also bonus even-if okay isn't-it?

example.jp: 言葉がなくても、気持ちは伝わる。
example.romaji: Kotoba ga nakute mo, kimochi wa tsutawaru.
example.en: Even without words, feelings get through.

lyrics.jp: 多分世界が次のフェーズへ行こうと見つけてみせるよ
lyrics.romaji: Tabun sekai ga tsugi no feezu e ikou to mitsukete miseru yo
lyrics.en: As the world moves on to the next phase, I'll make sure we find it

breakdown.meaning: will definitely find — just watch me (みつける + てみせる)

learn.grammar: Repeat of the key resolve line. 〜てみせる = strong determination. The same words now carry the full emotional weight of everything between verse 1 and the final chorus.
learn.literal: Probably world next phase toward going, will-definitely-find

example.jp: 絶対に見つけてみせるよ。
example.romaji: Zettai ni mitsukete miseru yo.
example.en: I'll definitely find it — just watch me.

lyrics.jp: いや嫌でも見つかるんだろうよ
lyrics.romaji: Iya, iya demo mitsukaru n darou yo
lyrics.en: Actually, it'll turn up whether we like it or not

breakdown.meaning: no, actually, well — discourse marker walking back the previous statement
whether we like it or not, inevitably
to be found, to turn up (intransitive)

learn.grammar: Repeat. The self-correction (いや — 'actually') gives vulnerability: 'I said I'd find it, but... it'll just be found on its own'. This humility is a core part of the song's emotional honesty.
learn.literal: Actually, even-if-reluctant, will-be-found probably (emphasis)

example.jp: 嫌でも、いつか向き合わなければならない。
example.romaji: Iya demo, itsuka mukiawa nakereba naranai.
example.en: Whether we like it or not, we'll have to face it someday.

lyrics.jp: あっけないくらい早い時間も良いんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Akkenai kurai hayai jikan mo ii n janai?
lyrics.en: Isn't it fine that time passes by so surprisingly fast?

breakdown.meaning: surprisingly brief, over before you know it, anticlimactically quick
to the extent of, as much as
isn't it okay? isn't it fine?

learn.grammar: Repeat. Near the end of the song, あっけない (surprisingly quick) takes on new weight — the whole song has also passed quickly, yet the tattoo endures.
learn.literal: Surprisingly-quick to-the-extent-of fast time also okay isn't-it?

example.jp: 楽しい時間はあっけないくらい過ぎてしまう。
example.romaji: Tanoshii jikan wa akkenai kurai sugite shimau.
example.en: Fun times pass by surprisingly quickly.

lyrics.jp: きっとありふれてないくらいが大切なんじゃない？
lyrics.romaji: Kitto arifurete nai kurai ga taisetsu na n janai?
lyrics.en: Surely something so uncommon is exactly what makes it precious, right?

breakdown.meaning: not commonplace, not ordinary, rare (negative of ありふれる)
it is precisely that extent which...

learn.grammar: Repeat. By the final chorus, ありふれてない (not commonplace) refers not to the world but to this specific bond — there is no other like it.
learn.literal: Surely not-commonplace extent is precious isn't-it?

example.jp: ありふれてないものだからこそ、大切にしたい。
example.romaji: Arifurete nai mono dakara koso, taisetsu ni shitai.
example.en: It's precisely because it's rare that I want to treasure it.

lyrics.jp: なんてさ本気で思っちゃう心の恥ずかしさが僕らだけにあるTATTOO
lyrics.romaji: Nante sa honki de omochau kokoro no hazukashisa ga bokura dake ni aru TATTOO
lyrics.en: Things like that — the embarrassment of genuinely believing it is a TATTOO that belongs only to us

breakdown.meaning: things like that, you know — casual dismissal + さ particle
can't help but think (〜てしまう contracted)
only in us, belonging to us alone
tattoo — the title, permanent mark, central metaphor

learn.grammar: The title line — final time. After the whole journey, this line is both conclusion and revelation. The 'embarrassment' of sincerely believing in something IS the tattoo: genuine feeling that cannot be erased.
learn.literal: Things like that — genuinely end-up-thinking, heart's embarrassment that only in us exists TATTOO

example.jp: 本気で思っちゃうことが、本物の証だ。
example.romaji: Honki de omochau koto ga, honmono no akashi da.
example.en: The things you can't help but genuinely believe are proof of something real.

lyrics.jp: 消えない 消さない 消させやしない
lyrics.romaji: Kienai kesanai kesase ya shinai
lyrics.en: Won't fade, won't erase it, won't let it be erased

breakdown.meaning: absolutely won't allow it to be erased (emphatic)

learn.grammar: Final repetition. The three-verb escalation (passive → active → causative) ends the song with total defiance. The tattoo — this embarrassing, irreplaceable, mutual sincerity — will not be touched.
learn.literal: Won't-fade, won't-erase, absolutely-won't-allow-to-be-erased

example.jp: 誰にも消させやしない。
example.romaji: Dare ni mo kesase ya shinai.
example.en: I won't let anyone erase it.

lyrics.jp: 大丈夫、 痛みにとっても弱いから
lyrics.romaji: Daijoubu, itami ni tottemo yowai kara
lyrics.en: It's okay, because I'm very weak when it comes to pain

learn.grammar: The outro returns to the opening line. Japanese storytelling values 循環 (cyclical structure): ending where you began signals transformation, not stagnation. 大丈夫 as an admission of weakness is now also a complete acceptance of it.
learn.literal: Okay — toward pain very weak, because

example.jp: 弱くてもいい。それが僕らだから。
example.romaji: Yowakute mo ii. Sore ga bokura dakara.
example.en: It's okay to be weak. That's just who we are.

lyrics.jp: 大丈夫、 憧れは今日も憧れのまんま
lyrics.romaji: Daijoubu, akogare wa kyou mo akogare no manma
lyrics.en: It's okay, even today our yearnings stay just as yearnings

learn.grammar: Subtle lyric change from the opening: 時に (sometimes) → 今日も (even today). One small word transforms the meaning entirely. The acceptance is no longer occasional — it's permanent.
learn.literal: Okay — yearning, even today, as yearning's as-is

example.jp: 今日も変わらず、あなたへの憧れは消えない。
example.romaji: Kyou mo kawarazu, anata e no akogare wa kienai.
example.en: Even today, unchanged, my longing for you won't fade.

vocab.meaning: relationship, the nature of how two people relate
to get complicated, to become tangled
bluster, putting on a brave face
surprisingly brief, over before you know it
